footer.painmapText: An educational triage tool. Not a substitute for clinical care.
footer.evidenceText: Every exercise cites a peer-reviewed source.

about.problemP1: For most desk workers, aches are mechanical — and the right exercise resolves them. The hard part is finding which one.
about.approachP1: Click where it hurts. Get the single resistance-band exercise with the strongest evidence — dosage, contraindications, a citation, and a video. No upsell, no ads.

legal.disclaimerP1Pre: PainMap is an educational tool. It is
legal.disclaimerP1Post: and is not intended to diagnose or treat any condition.
legal.disclaimerP2: Check with a clinician before starting — especially if you have:
disclaimerRedFlags: Numbness, tingling, or weakness radiating into the limbs
Recent trauma, fever, or unexplained weight loss
Pain that worsens with rest or wakes you from sleep
legal.privacyP1: Runs entirely in your browser — no personal data, no tracking cookies. Code is MIT-licensed; content © PainMap. Provided "as is", no warranty.

physio.sub: Physiotherapy & sports-therapy clinics in Tel Aviv.
physio.redFlagNote: Have red-flag symptoms — numbness, recent trauma, fever, or night pain? See a doctor before exercising.
physio.disclaimer: Publicly listed clinics, not endorsements — please confirm availability and pricing directly. More cities coming.

clinician.introP: Red-flag symptoms, or pain that doesn't fit the usual mechanical pattern? See a clinician first.
clinician.israelP1: Physical therapy is covered by all four health funds with a primary-care referral:

hero.title: Office pain relief, personalized.
hero.sub: Pick where it hurts, answer a few focused questions, and get one evidence-based resistance-band routine with clear safety guidance.

trust.title: What PainMap does and does not do
does: Suggests evidence-based resistance-band exercises by pain location.
Builds a simple, safety-first routine for common desk-related pain.
Lets you track weekly adherence and symptom trend.
not: It does not diagnose medical conditions.
It does not replace a clinician evaluation.
It should not be used for trauma or red-flag symptoms.
trust.footnote: Need source details? Review the clinical references in the Evidence page.

bodyArea.heading: Where does it hurt?
bodyArea.sub: Pick a region, then the exact spot.
bodyArea.next: Your exercise opens right away — with a short video demo.

assessment.heading: Quick discomfort intake

redFlag.nightPain: Night pain waking you from sleep

assessment.redFlagWarning: One or more red flags were selected. Pause self-guided exercise and see a clinician.

routine.heading: Your plan
routine.feelText: Gentle muscular effort around the target area, not sharp or radiating pain.
routine.safetyText: Stop if you feel sharp, radiating, or worsening pain.
routine.safetyText2: If symptoms worsen after exercise, reduce load next time or pause and consult a clinician.
routine.openCard: Open full exercise card (detailed cues + citation)

dosage.low: 2 sets, light band tension, smooth tempo.
dosage.medium: 2-3 sets, moderate tension, stop with 2 reps in reserve.
dosage.high: 3 sets, progressive tension, full controlled range.

warmup.mobility: Start with 2 minutes of gentle joint mobility before loading.
warmup.default: Start with 60-90 seconds of dynamic warm-up for the target area.

cooldown.referral: Finish with low-load breathing and symptom check before stopping.
cooldown.default: Finish with 1-2 minutes of light stretching and breathing.

routine.progressionRule: If pain stays <= 3/10 during and after sessions for 3 consecutive days, increase resistance one level or add 1 set.

low.easier: Reduce range of motion and use a lighter band.
low.harder: Add a pause at end range before returning slowly.

medium.easier: Drop one set and keep tempo smooth with light tension.
medium.harder: Add one set or progress one band color when pain stays <= 3/10.

high.easier: Use one lighter band and shorter set duration for the first week.
high.harder: Increase resistance one level and add eccentric control (3 sec lowering).

equipment.bandOnly: Start with a light resistance band set (yellow + red) and a door anchor.
equipment.bandAndChair: Add one medium resistance band (green) for progression.
equipment.fullSet: Keep your full band set sorted by color and log your working resistance.

desk.gt8: Raise monitor to eye level, keep elbows near 90°, and add lumbar support.
desk.default: Keep monitor centered, feet grounded, and keyboard close to reduce shoulder load.

break.rarely: Use a 30/2 protocol: every 30 minutes, take a 2-minute movement break.
break.1to2: Upgrade to 3 movement breaks per work block with neck/shoulder resets.
break.default: Maintain your current break habit and anchor one mobility drill per break.
